headline: Is Veralto (VLTO) A Bargain On Its Russell Growth Index Exit?
summary: Index changes put Veralto in focus Veralto (VLTO) recently dropped from several Russell growth benchmarks, including the Russell 1000 Growth and Russell Midcap Growth indices. This change is putting new attention on how the stock fits into diversified portfolios. See our latest analysis for Veralto. Veralto’s recent removal from several Russell growth indices comes after a mixed price pattern, with a 30-day share price return of 8.19% and a year to date share price return that is down 5.48%,...

headline: Here's Why You Should Hold Omnicom Group Stock in Your Portfolio
summary: OMC's broad service portfolio, AI platform expansion and major client wins support growth. Its competition and liquidity remain key risks.

headline: Veralto (VLTO) Stock Trades At A Discount On Its 9% Slide
summary: Veralto stock has slipped around 8.5% over the past year, yet its Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) intrinsic value estimate points to meaningful upside while market multiples suggest the shares already trade at a premium. This leaves investors with a clear valuation split to weigh. Over the last 12 months, Veralto has delivered a decline of 8.5%, which frames the current share price as a pullback rather than a momentum story. The valuation outlook can be supported if Veralto converts a larger...

headline: Is Veralto (VLTO) Trading at a Fair Valuation?
summary: Artisan Partners, an investment management firm, issued its first-quarter 2026 investor letter for the “Artisan Mid Cap Value Fund”. A copy of this letter is available for download here. In Q1 2026, the portfolio underperformed the benchmark Russell Midcap Value Index as the market favored momentum-driven stocks over quality factors.
